ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a Fanta-specific entry; FOODON_03301710 (carbonated soft drink) is the closest defensible parent match for a fruit-flavoured carbonated sugar-sweetened beverage.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKC covers carbonated soft drinks broadly; no Fanta-specific code exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy. A0FKC is used as the closest parent match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for Fanta or fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drink at this time.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve can of Fanta is 330 ml, delivering approximately 155 kcal and 36.6 g of sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Fanta (any flavour) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D06020012 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. Values are accepted as-is. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, potassium, vitamin C, and other micronutrients not reported by HPB for this entry are set to -1 (data not available in source).

gi_source.title: Soft drink, Fanta, orange — Glycemic Index entry; analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened carbonated soft drink GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No single Fanta-all-flavours GI entry exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 68 is estimated from published GI values for sugar-sweetened carbonated soft drinks (range 59–72, central estimate ~68) as documented in the University of Sydney GI Database for cola and orange soft drink entries. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (68) × carbohydrate per serving (36.6 g from 330 ml) / 100 = 24.9, rounded to 25.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (11.09 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention, despite very low sodium (3 mg/100 g).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Fanta entirely and replace it with plain water, sparkling water, or unsweetened beverages, as the AHA 2018 guidelines identify SSBs as an independent cardiovascular and blood-pressure risk factor regardless of sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Fanta is a sugar-sweetened beverage delivering 11.09 g of rapidly absorbed free sugar per 100 g with zero dietary fibre, causing acute postprandial blood glucose spikes with no fibre modulation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Fanta entirely; a standard 330 ml can delivers approximately 36.6 g of free sugar with a glycemic load of ~25, making it incompatible with blood glucose management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, weight gain, and elevated cardiovascular disease risk independent of other dietary factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should eliminate Fanta and other SSBs from their diet, as habitual SSB consumption is linked to dyslipidaemia, visceral adiposity, and increased CVD mortality per meta-analyses in the American Journal of Clinical Nutrition.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar content (11.09 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity with no anti-inflammatory nutrients present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Fanta entirely and replace it with anti-inflammatory beverages such as green tea or plain water, as high sugar intake exacerbates systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fanta (Any Flavour) is a globally distributed ultra-processed carbonated fruit-flavoured soft drink manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company. It is available in a wide range of fruit flavours including orange, grape, strawberry, and lemon, all sharing a broadly similar nutritional profile. The beverage is produced by combining carbonated water with sugar, fruit flavourings, citric acid, food colourings, and preservatives. It contains no protein, no fat, and no dietary fibre; its entire caloric content of 47 kcal per 100 g derives from 11.09 g of free sugar per 100 g. Sodium content is very low at 3 mg per 100 g. A standard 330 ml can delivers approximately 155 kcal and 36.6 g of free sugar. Clinically, Fanta is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is contraindicated for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. The liquid sugar matrix is absorbed rapidly with no fibre to moderate glycaemic response, producing a glycemic index of approximately 68 (medium) and a glycemic load of ~25 per standard serving. The AHA, WHO, and Singapore HPB all recommend limiting or eliminating SSB consumption. Fanta is widely consumed in Singapore and globally as a casual refreshment and is commonly available in cans, bottles, and fountain dispensers.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in Fanta?
What flavours does Fanta come in?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D06020012, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous soft drink entries with documented basis. All core macros and secondary nutrients are complete and consistent with the source.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero: Fanta is a sugar-water carbonated beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation; Fanta contains no fat-bearing ingredients. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero; no fibre-containing ingredients are present. calcium_mg and iron_mg are confirmed zero per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these fields are not reported by HPB Singapore Foodo for this entry and are not available in the source as of 2026-04-16. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database soft drink entries; no single Fanta-all-flavours GI value is published. Fanta is not a Singaporean dish; multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable and are set to null. The record covers all Fanta flavours generically; individual flavour variants may have minor nutritional differences. Ontology match types corrected to closest_parent for both FoodOn and FoodEx2, as these are the nearest parent category codes rather than exact matches for this specific branded product.
